title: Polestar Q1 2026 Earnings: Key Takeaways from Earnings Call
text: Polestar reported mixed results for Q1 2026 with concerning profitability metrics. Revenue held steady at $633 million, net loss jumped to $383 million from $166 million in Q1 2025, and gross margin deteriorated to negative 3.2%. Adjusted EBITDA loss widened to $235 million versus $96 million year-over-year. Cash position declined to $676 million at quarter-end. Operationally, the company achieved positive volume metrics despite margin challenges. Retail sales reached 13,100+ vehicles with 7% growth. Europe drove momentum with 78% of sales and 11% regional growth, with particularly strong performance in UK (20%), Germany (35%), and Sweden (17%). Polestar 4 represented 67% of Q1 volume versus 49% in prior year. Management outlined transformation efforts including expanding from 150 to approximately 250 sales points globally by end-2026, consolidating manufacturing footprint to reduce tariff exposure, staff reductions of approximately 25% to 1,700 employees, and planned shift to regional manufacturing including Polestar 7 production in Europe. Key challenges include pricing pressure from intensified EV competition, EU and U.S. tariff impacts on profitability, decline in carbon credit revenue, and unfavorable product mix.
